figures.caption: Fig. 1: (a) CAD construction is performed through a sequence of discrete commands (for clarity, parameters are omitted). (b) Existing continuous diffusion methods embed discrete commands into a continuous space and perturb them with isotropic Gaussian noise, which may result in invalid or semantically meaningless commands. (c) The proposed discrete diffusion operates directly over discrete token probabilities, perturbing only meaningful symbols and preserving semantic consistency.
Fig. 5: Visualization of CAD models using ground truth commands.
TABLE II: Comparison with state-of-the-art methods. Best results are highlighted in bold.
Fig. 4: Visualization of CAD models generated by continuous diffusion and discrete diffusion.

Fig. 2: Pipeline of the proposed cascaded discrete diffusion model (CDDM) for CAD generation. The generation process consists of two sequential stages: the first stage produces discrete CAD commands through a command diffusion model, and the second stage reconstructs the corresponding CAD parameters using a conditional discrete diffusion model conditioned on the generated commands.
Fig. 6: Visualization of generated CAD models under the condition of command length.

Fig. 3: Schematic of the attention mask used for commandlevel local attention. (a) illustrates the flattened parameter sequence corresponding to different CAD commands. (b) shows the local attention mask, where colored cells indicate valid interactions (mask value Mij = 0) and blank cells indicate masked positions (mask value Mij = −∞).
Fig. 7: Visualization of generated CAD models under the condition of point clouds.
